test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_ecommerce
test_cases.scenario: Complete EDA plan generation for a typical e-commerce dataset with 50k rows, mixed column types, and standard analysis scope.

input.dataset_description: E-commerce sales data with 50k rows: date (datetime), product_id (int), revenue (float), region (string), customer_segment (categorical: premium/standard/budget), quantity (int), discount_applied (boolean)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates comprehensive EDA plan with all 9 outputs populated, covering all 7 columns, identifying 2% missing revenue as MCAR, recommending appropriate statistical tests, and producing 3-5 testable hypotheses.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests

test_cases.name: happy_path_quick_scope
test_cases.scenario: Quick EDA plan for a small healthcare dataset with 5k rows and limited analysis scope.

input.dataset_description: Patient health records with 5k rows: patient_id (int), age (int), blood_pressure_systolic (int), blood_pressure_diastolic (int), cholesterol (float), diagnosis (categorical), treatment_date (datetime)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates focused EDA plan with top 5 insights, skips advanced feature engineering, recommends simple transformations only, and provides realistic effort estimates under 10 hours.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests

test_cases.name: happy_path_deep_scope
test_cases.scenario: Deep EDA plan for a climate science dataset with 100k rows requiring advanced multivariate analysis and feature engineering.

input.dataset_description: Climate monitoring data with 100k rows: timestamp (datetime), temperature (float), humidity (float), pressure (float), wind_speed (float), precipitation (float), location (categorical: 50 stations), season (categorical)
known_constraints: wind_speed occasionally missing during sensor maintenance

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates comprehensive EDA plan with advanced multivariate methods, recommends isolation forest for outlier detection, includes lag features and rolling statistics for temporal patterns, and suggests interaction terms for non-linear relationships.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests

test_cases.name: edge_case_small_dataset
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan for a very small dataset with only 100 rows and 3 columns, testing power analysis and statistical test selection.

input.dataset_description: Small clinical trial data with 100 rows: treatment_group (categorical: control/treatment), outcome (numeric: 0-100), age (numeric)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates EDA plan that explicitly notes small sample size (<1k rows), recommends conservative significance thresholds (α=0.01), emphasizes effect size reporting over p-values, and flags power limitations.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests

test_cases.name: edge_case_large_dataset
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan for a very large dataset with 5M rows, testing practical significance thresholds and visualization prioritization.

input.dataset_description: Large web analytics data with 5M rows: user_id (int), session_duration (float), pages_visited (int), conversion (boolean), device_type (categorical: mobile/desktop/tablet), country (categorical: 150 countries)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates EDA plan that acknowledges large sample size (>1M rows), recommends practical significance thresholds over statistical significance, prioritizes top 10 visualizations with dashboard structure, and notes that even tiny effects will be statistically significant.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests

test_cases.name: edge_case_high_cardinality_categorical
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan for dataset with high-cardinality categorical column (500+ unique values), testing dimensionality reduction recommendations.

input.dataset_description: Product catalog data with 10k rows: product_id (int, 8000 unique values), product_name (text, 8000 unique values), category (categorical: 50 categories), price (float), sales_volume (int)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates EDA plan that identifies high-cardinality columns, recommends dimensionality reduction techniques (target encoding, clustering, grouping), and suggests feature engineering to reduce feature space from 8000 to manageable size.

test_cases.name: error_handling_missing_column_types
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan generation when dataset description lacks explicit data types, testing assumption documentation.

input.dataset_description: Sales data with 20k rows: date, product_id, revenue, region, customer_segment

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates EDA plan with inferred data types, documents assumptions made about missing type information, and flags columns for verification with domain expert.

test_cases.name: error_handling_high_missing_values
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan when multiple columns have >20% missing values, testing missing value strategy prioritization.

input.dataset_description: Survey data with 5k rows: respondent_id (int), age (int), income (float), education (categorical), satisfaction_score (int), comments (text)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates missing value plan that recommends excluding or creating missing indicators for columns with >20% nulls, investigates missingness mechanisms (MAR/MNAR), and documents reversible strategies.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_visualization_count_exceeds_20
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan for dataset with many columns (15 numeric, 10 categorical) producing >20 visualization recommendations, testing dashboard structure recommendation.

input.dataset_description: Financial dataset with 50k rows: date, open_price, close_price, high_price, low_price, volume, market_cap, pe_ratio, dividend_yield, earnings_growth, revenue_growth, debt_ratio, current_ratio, roa, roe, sector, country, industry, company_size, risk_rating

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates visualization recommendations exceeding 20 items, prioritizes top 10 by target variable involvement and correlation strength, recommends dashboard structure with sections (univariate, bivariate, multivariate, outliers).
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests

test_cases.name: quality_gate_hypothesis_generation_grounded
test_cases.scenario: EDA plan generates 3-5 testable hypotheses grounded in correlation and distribution findings, not speculative.

input.dataset_description: Marketing campaign data with 30k rows: campaign_id (int), spend (float), impressions (int), clicks (int), conversions (int), audience_segment (categorical: A/B/C), channel (categorical: email/social/search), ctr (float), conversion_rate (float)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Generates 3-5 testable hypotheses (e.g., 'spend positively predicts conversions; Pearson r > 0.5'), each grounded in correlation/distribution findings, specifies confirmation test, and prioritizes by business impact.
phase_coverage: Phase 4: Plan Correlation and Relationship Tests
